assertions.title: Finance and reconciliation consume dispatch truth instead of redefining it

steps.action: Read the validation contract and Lane 03 boundary note
steps.expected: Find the canonical dispatch/accounting boundary and downstream consumers
steps.observed: The contract and decision note state dispatch confirmation is the canonical sales-side accounting truth boundary and that reconciliation/period close consume that model.

steps.action: Run the targeted accounting JVM proof pack
steps.expected: CriticalAccountingAxesIT and related accounting proof stay green
steps.observed: BUILD SUCCESS; CriticalAccountingAxesIT passed 12 tests and ReconciliationControlsIT passed 3 tests in the targeted accounting run.

steps.expected: Dispatch confirmation posts COGS/AR and creates the invoice without alternate posting boundaries
steps.observed: SalesCoreEngine.confirmDispatch calls accountingFacade.postCogsJournal and accountingFacade.postSalesJournal, then creates/syncs the invoice and ledger markers.

assertions.title: Accounting cleanup preserves dependent-module behavior without duplicating accounting seams

steps.action: Run the targeted dependent proof pack
steps.expected: Sales, inventory, purchasing, invoice, and period-close dependent flows stay green
steps.observed: BUILD SUCCESS; OrderFulfillmentE2ETest, ProcureToPayE2ETest, InventoryAccountingEventListenerIT, CreditDebitNoteIT, ReconciliationControlsIT, TS_PeriodCloseBoundaryCoverageTest, and TS_PeriodCloseAtomicSnapshotTest all passed.

steps.expected: Touched areas rely on the canonical accounting facade instead of alternate write helpers
steps.observed: Dependent modules route through AccountingFacade; the only super.* hits in accounting services are thin delegating wrappers, not a second posting implementation.

steps.action: Check the source evidence for the dependent proof surfaces
steps.expected: No duplicate helper seam or fallback accounting writer remains in the touched paths
steps.observed: Sales, purchasing, inventory, reconciliation, and period-close flows all resolve through canonical facade/core entrypoints, with no alternate write boundary found in the scanned touched areas.

frictions.description: The Maven terminal output was truncated, so exact per-class results were verified from Surefire summary files under target/surefire-reports.
frictions.resolution: Read the generated Surefire .txt summaries for each target test class and used those summaries as the authoritative proof record.

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 2 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Dispatch confirmation remains the canonical sales-side accounting truth boundary, and the dependent sales, inventory, purchasing, invoice, reconciliation, and period-close flows stayed green without a duplicate accounting fallback path.
